titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/231

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=355 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:40:44.427656+00 | translation moa=356 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:40:45.914978+00

div.p:
. A . Dei Gratia Rex scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem . Sciatis nos concessisse et hac carta nostra confirmasse uendi
tionem illam quam . P . Comes de Dunbar’ de consensu Patricii . filii et heredis sui fecit abbati et Conuentuj de Melros’ de toto equitio
suo quod habuit in feodo de Lauhedir’ . Anno gratie Millesimo . Ducentesimo . Quadragesimo septimo in festo decollationis sancti Iohannis Baptiste . Et
similiter concessimus et hac carta nostra confirmauimus concessionem et quietam clamationem quam Idem Comes de consensu dicti . P . filii et heredis sui
fecit eisdem abbati et Conuentui de communa quam habuit ad equitium uel ad aliqua alia animalia nutrienda uel pascenda infra terras et pastu
ras quas dicti abbas et Conuentus in feodo de Lauhedir’ dicto die tenuerunt et possederunte . Ita scilicet quod dictus Comes aut heredes sui uel Aliquis
alius nomine eorum nullum clamium aut calumpniam in dictis terris aut pasturis decetero exigent aut habebunt . quam quidem uenditionem et quietam
clamationem ita uolumus in perpetuum obseruari : sicut carta predicti Patricii Comitis . de Dunbar’ . quam dicti abbas et Conuentus inde habent . iuste testatur .
Testibus . W . Cumin Comite . de Meneteth’ . Alano hostiario . Iustic’ . scocie . Roberto . de Ros’ . Dauid de Lindes’ . Thoma de Normanuill’ . Thoma
filio Ranulfi . Roberto . de Meiners’ . apud sanctam crucem Vicesimo Octauo die Nouembris . anno regni domini regis . Tricesimo tertio .

Alexander by the grace of God king of the Scots sends greeting to all worthy men of the whole of his land. Know us to have allowed, and by this our charter to have made firm, the sale that Patrick earl of Dunbar, by the agreement of Patrick his son and heir, made to the abbot and convent of Melrose of his whole horse-stud that he had in the feu of Lauder in the Year of Grace One Thousand Two Hundred and Forty-Seven on the feast of the Beheading of John the Baptist. And we have likewise allowed, and by this our charter made firm, the concession and quitclaim that the same earl, with the agreement of the said Patrick his son and heir, made to the same abbot and convent for the common that he had for feeding or pasturing the horse-stud or any other animals within the lands and pastures which the said abbot and convent held and possessed on the said day in the said feu of Lauder, that is, so the said earl or his heirs or anyone else in their name shall in future instigate or have no claim or complaint in the said lands or pastures; which very sale and quitclaim we will to be observed for ever, just as the charter of the foresaid Patrick earl of Dunbar which the said abbot and convent have thereupon rightly bears witness. As witnesses, William Comyn, earl of Menteith; Alan Durward, justiciar of Scotia; Robert of Roos; David of Lindsay; Thomas de Normanville; Thomas son of Ranulph; Robert Menzies. At Holyrood on the twenty-eighth day of November, in the thirty-third year of the reign of the lord King. [1247]
